headline: TSMC Forecasts Sustained AI Chip Demand
summary: TSM stock rose on Thursday after the company's annual shareholders' meeting.  At the meeting, Chief Executive C.C. Wei said the contract chipmaker, better known as TSMC, won't be able to fulfill demand over the next few years, even as more manufacturing capacity comes online, Bloomberg reported.  "It will be a long time before we can meet customer demand," Wei said.

"It will be a long time before we can meet customer demand," Wei said.

headline: Intel Stock Drops After It Scores AI Partnership With Apple Supplier Foxconn
summary: Intel  is teaming up with Apple supplier Foxconn to develop artificial-intelligence infrastructure—but that wasn’t enough to stop the chip manufacturer’s stock from sliding on Thursday.  The selloff came after Intel and Foxconn, also known as  Hon Hai Precision Industry  said they would collaborate to speed up the AI buildout.  “Together, we are accelerating the delivery of end-to-end platforms that unlock new capabilities and extend the impact of AI worldwide,” Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan said.

headline: TSMC boss bets big on AI growth, says he'd 'like' to hike chip prices
summary: STORY: The world's largest contract chipmaker TSMC says it's confident in its growth over the next few years...thanks to robust demand for computing power and advanced semiconductors as it rides a relentless AI boom.The Taiwanese firm's CEO spoke at its annual shareholders meeting on Thursday.C.C. Wei said customers are still positive on the outlook for AI, although the company continues to monitor the impact of rising component costs.Asked whether TSMC could raise prices for customers, Wei said the firm would like to do that as, quote, "we still need to make money."Taiwan plays a crucial role in the global AI supply chain for companies including Nvidia  and Apple.And its position is anchored by TSMC.The tech giant said it was working hard to meet all customer demands...although added that it will take a "very long time" to fully satisfy American customers' needs with production in the U.S.Looking ahead, Wei said he views autonomous vehicles as long-term growth drivers.But TSMC faces political risk as China steps up military pressure on democratically and separately governed Taiwan...which Beijing views as Chinese territory.

raw.headline: TSMC boss bets big on AI growth, says he'd 'like' to hike chip prices
raw.summary: STORY: The world's largest contract chipmaker TSMC says it's confident in its growth over the next few years...thanks to robust demand for computing power and advanced semiconductors as it rides a relentless AI boom.The Taiwanese firm's CEO spoke at its annual shareholders meeting on Thursday.C.C. Wei said customers are still positive on the outlook for AI, although the company continues to monitor the impact of rising component costs.Asked whether TSMC could raise prices for customers, Wei said the firm would like to do that as, quote, "we still need to make money."Taiwan plays a crucial role in the global AI supply chain for companies including Nvidia  and Apple.And its position is anchored by TSMC.The tech giant said it was working hard to meet all customer demands...although added that it will take a "very long time" to fully satisfy American customers' needs with production in the U.S.Looking ahead, Wei said he views autonomous vehicles as long-term growth drivers.But TSMC faces political risk as China steps up military pressure on democratically and separately governed Taiwan...which Beijing views as Chinese territory.
